parameters.content:
# Monitor Multiple Github Repos
This workflow allows you to monitor multiple Github repos simultaneously without polling due to use of Webhooks. It programmatically allows for adding and deleting of repos to your watchlist to make management convenient.

nodes.name: Repos to Monitor
nodes.notes: This set node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note7

parameters.content:
## Test
## 1. Register Webhooks
- In `Repos to Monitor`, add any repo you want to monitor changes for. 
- Disable `Webhook Trigger`, Click `Test Workflow` and if your Github credentials were set correctly, it will automatically register the webhooks. - You can test this by running the single node `Get Existing Webhook` and confirming it outputs the repo addresses.
## 2. Handle Github Events
- Now that you have registered the webhooks, reenable `Webhook Trigger` and activate the workflow.
- Make a commit to any of the registered repos.
- Confirm that the notification went through.
*That's it!*

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note8

parameters.content:
## Setup
## 1. Creating Credentials on Github
#### Generate a personal access token on github by following these esteps;
- Right hand side of page -> Settings -> scroll to bottom -> Developer Settings > Personal Access Token > Tokens (classic) > Generate New Token
- Give scopes:
   *admin:repo_hook*
   *repo* (if you want to use it for your own private repo)

if you need more help, see here:
{{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

## 2. Setting Credentials in n8n
In `Register Github Webhook`
*Authenticaion > Generic Credential Type*
*Generic Auth Type > Header Auth*
*Header Auth > Create New Credential* with Name set to *'Authorization'* and Value set to *'Bearer <YOUR GITHUB TOKEN HERE>'*.
(You can reuse this for `Delete Github Webhook` and `Get Existing Webhooks`).
Now in `Register Github Webhook`, scroll down to Send Body > JSON and inside the JSON, change the value of *"url"* to the webhook address given as Production URL in the node `Webhook Trigger`.


## 3. Notification settings
In the third row, link up the Webhook Trigger to any API of your choice. Slack and Telegram are given as examples.
You can also format the notification message as you wish.

parameters.content:
# Monitor Multiple Github Repos
This workflow allows you to monitor multiple Github repos simultaneously without polling due to use of Webhooks. It programmatically allows for adding and deleting of repos to your watchlist to make management convenient.

## Test
## 1. Register Webhooks
- In `Repos to Monitor`, add any repo you want to monitor changes for. 
- Disable `Webhook Trigger`, Click `Test Workflow` and if your Github credentials were set correctly, it will automatically register the webhooks. - You can test this by running the single node `Get Existing Webhook` and confirming it outputs the repo addresses.
## 2. Handle Github Events
- Now that you have registered the webhooks, reenable `Webhook Trigger` and activate the workflow.
- Make a commit to any of the registered repos.
- Confirm that the notification went through.
*That's it!*

## Setup
## 1. Creating Credentials on Github
#### Generate a personal access token on github by following these esteps;
- Right hand side of page -> Settings -> scroll to bottom -> Developer Settings > Personal Access Token > Tokens (classic) > Generate New Token
- Give scopes:
   *admin:repo_hook*
   *repo* (if you want to use it for your own private repo)

if you need more help, see here:
{{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

## 2. Setting Credentials in n8n
In `Register Github Webhook`
*Authenticaion > Generic Credential Type*
*Generic Auth Type > Header Auth*
*Header Auth > Create New Credential* with Name set to *'Authorization'* and Value set to *'Bearer <YOUR GITHUB TOKEN HERE>'*.
(You can reuse this for `Delete Github Webhook` and `Get Existing Webhooks`).
Now in `Register Github Webhook`, scroll down to Send Body > JSON and inside the JSON, change the value of *"url"* to the webhook address given as Production URL in the node `Webhook Trigger`.


## 3. Notification settings
In the third row, link up the Webhook Trigger to any API of your choice. Slack and Telegram are given as examples.
You can also format the notification message as you wish.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking ‘Test workflow’".
Locate the webhook node named "Webhook Trigger".
Copy the Test Webhook URL provided by n8n.
Configure your source application (e.g. Stripe, Typeform) to send event payloads to this URL.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Split Out" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Register Github Webhook" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Split Out1" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Delete Github Webhook" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note6" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Notify Slack" Slack node.
Select the Channel or User ID where message notifications should be delivered.
Open the "Telegram" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note7" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note8" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Existing Hook" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Fields".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Repos to Monitor".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Repos to Monitor1".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Hook URL".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Your Slack channel will receive customized real-time notifications when the automation executes.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
